pages.raw_text:
Several of the local unions have
taken action for the purpose of rem
|dering what assistance they can to
the San Francisco sufferers. The
bartenders have telegraphed to the
San Francisco local rendering finan-
cial aid. The carpenters will give
ball, and federal union No. 11624
at it's meeting last Saturday night
voted the sum of 800, and instruct
ed the secretary to telegraph the
money. 1t is probable that other
unions will follow with what am
cunts they can afford.


Federal No. 1924 had a rousing
meeting last Saturday evening, q
new members being initiated. The
large amount of dues collected
showed the union to be in a most
prosperous condition. The dues paid
in reached a total of more than
500. The union decided to accept
the cards of all union men in this
city whose international was affil-
lated with the American Federation
of Labor, but who did not have a
local in Spokane. Twenty-Five ap.
plications for membership were al.
SO received.
